activities_projects.title: Design a Simple Aquarium Model (NCERT Activity 13.1 inspired)
materials: Water, small plants (like algae or water plants if available)
Small pebbles, fish food picture or toy fish
steps: Fill jar with water and add pebbles at bottom
Add green plants or draw them
Place toy fish or draw fish, snails
Label biotic (living) and abiotic (non-living) parts
Discuss what keeps it balanced
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns biotic and abiotic components of ecosystem
activities_projects.evaluation: Model labels and short explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Draw a Food Chain Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn about energy flow in food chain
steps: Choose a simple food chain (e.g., grass → grasshopper → frog → snake)
Draw organisms and connect with arrows
Label producer, herbivore, carnivore
Write one sentence on energy flow
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands basic food chain and roles
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of chain and neat presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Waste Classification List
steps: List 10 items from home/school (e.g., banana peel, plastic bag)
Mark each as biodegradable or non-biodegradable
Note why some take longer to decompose
Discuss importance of segregation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes waste types and environmental impact

activities_projects.title: Ozone Layer Simple Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about ozone and its protection
steps: Draw Earth with atmosphere layers
Color ozone layer in blue
Draw arrows showing CFC damage and UV rays
Write 3 effects of ozone depletion and 2 ways to protect
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands ozone's role and depletion causes
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram clarity and key points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: My Daily Waste Diary
steps: For one day, list all waste items you throw away
Count biodegradable vs non-biodegradable
Suggest one way to reduce waste
Share with class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Becomes aware of personal environmental impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of diary and suggestion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem in a Garden Observation
steps: Visit school garden or home plant area
List 5 living things (plants, insects, birds)
List 5 non-living things (soil, air, sunlight)
Draw one interaction (e.g., bee on flower)

activities_projects.title: Save the Environment Slogan and Poster
steps: Create 5 catchy slogans (e.g., 'Reduce Plastic, Save Planet')
Draw a simple poster with one slogan
Explain why environment needs protection

activities_projects.title: Food Web vs Food Chain Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Understand difference between chain and web
steps: Draw a simple food chain
Add more arrows to make it a food web
Write 2 differences
Explain why web is more stable

activities_projects.title: Environment Quiz Game
steps: Prepare 10 easy questions (e.g., What is biodegradable?)
Play quiz with friends or family
Note correct answers

activities_projects.title: Idea to Reduce Plastic Use
steps: Think of one problem caused by plastic
Draw or write a simple idea to reduce it (e.g., cloth bag)
Explain how it helps environment
Present to class
